enonce.texte:
Read the following text about conflict resolution in the family and answer the questions below.

Family Conflict Resolution

In many families, conflicts arise from misunderstandings or differences in opinions. When conflicts occur, it is important to resolve them peacefully. Family members should listen to each other and express their feelings calmly. They can discuss the problem together and find a solution that satisfies everyone. Sometimes, they need to compromise to maintain harmony in the family.

questions.texte: What are the key actions family members should take to resolve conflicts according to the text?
questions.reponse: According to the text, family members should listen to each other, express their feelings calmly, discuss the problem together, find a solution, and compromise.

questions.texte: Define the term 'compromise' in your own words.
questions.reponse: Compromise means finding a middle ground or agreement where each person gives up something to reach a solution that is acceptable to everyone.

enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences with the correct reciprocal pronoun (each other, one another, one another's).

questions.texte: The two brothers always help ______ when they have problems.
In a family, members should respect ______ opinions.
During the conflict resolution meeting, all participants listened to ______.
Students in the school should support ______ in conflict situations.

enonce.texte:
Read the following dialogue between a mother (Mrs. Mbarga) and her son (Kevin) about a family conflict. Then answer the questions.

Dialogue:

Mrs. Mbarga: Kevin, why haven't you done your homework yet?

Kevin: I'm sorry, Mom. I was playing with my friends.

Mrs. Mbarga: You know your father and I expect you to finish your homework before playing.

Kevin: I understand, but I had promised my friends I would play with them.

Mrs. Mbarga: We need to find a solution. How about you do your homework now, and tomorrow you can play with your friends?

Kevin: That sounds fair. I will do my homework right now.

questions.texte: Identify the conflict in this dialogue.
questions.reponse: The conflict is about Kevin not doing his homework because he was playing with friends, while his parents expect him to finish homework before playing.

questions.texte: What solution does Mrs. Mbarga propose?
questions.reponse: Mrs. Mbarga proposes that Kevin does his homework now and can play with his friends tomorrow.

questions.texte: Evaluate the effectiveness of the solution proposed by Mrs. Mbarga. Do you think it is fair? Why or why not?
questions.reponse: Yes, I think it is fair because it allows Kevin to fulfill both his responsibilities (homework) and his commitment to his friends. It also teaches him the importance of prioritizing tasks and finding a balance.

enonce.texte:
Create a dialogue between two siblings (John and Mary) who are having a conflict about watching television. One wants to watch a football match while the other wants to watch a movie. Include the following elements:

  • Use at least one reciprocal pronoun (each other, one another, one another's).
  • Show how they resolve their conflict peacefully.
  • Include suggestions or compromise.

questions.texte: Write the dialogue between John and Mary.
questions.reponse:
John: Mary, I really want to watch the football match now.
Mary: But I've been waiting to watch this movie for a long time!
John: We can't watch both at the same time.
Mary: Why don't we compromise? You can watch the football match for one hour, and then I can watch my movie for one hour.
John: That sounds fair. We should respect one another's preferences.
Mary: Yes, we need to understand each other better.
John: I agree. Let's do this: I'll watch the first half, and you can watch the movie during the second half.
Mary: Perfect! We resolved our conflict peacefully!

enonce.texte:
Your friend, Paul, is having a conflict with his parents. He wants to go on a trip with his friends, but his parents think he is too young to travel alone. Write a letter to Paul giving him advice on how to resolve this conflict. In your letter, include:

  • Advice on how to express his feelings respectfully.
  • Suggestions for compromise.
  • Examples of reciprocal pronouns (each other, one another, one another's).

questions.reponse:
Dear Paul,

I hope this letter finds you well. I understand you're having a conflict with your parents about going on a trip with your friends. This is a common situation that many teenagers face.

First, try to express your feelings to your parents calmly. Tell them why this trip is important to you and how much you would like to go. Listen to their concerns and try to understand their worries. You should respect one another's opinions.

Second, propose a compromise. For example, you could suggest going on a shorter trip, or promise to call them every day. Show them that you are responsible and can take care of yourself. You can also ask them to meet your friends' parents so they feel more comfortable.

Remember, resolving conflicts peacefully requires both sides to understand each other. By being patient and respectful, you and your parents can find a solution that works for everyone.

I hope this advice helps you. Good luck!

Your friend,
[Your Name]
